{"filepath": "src/System/Agents/OS/Interfaces.hs", "content": "{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedRecordDot #-}
{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}

{- | Common interface abstraction for TUI and OneShot interfaces.

This module provides a unified interface that both TUI and OneShot can use
to interact with the OS. The interface uses OS-native structures.
-}
module System.Agents.OS.Interfaces (
    -- * Interface Handle
    InterfaceHandle (..),
    InterfaceConfig (..),

    -- * Interface Operations (via typeclass)
    OSInterface (..),

    -- * Agent Operations
    AgentHandle (..),
    AgentStatus (..),
    createAgent,
    destroyAgent,
    sendMessage,
    receiveMessage,

    -- * Conversation Operations
    ConversationHandle (..),
    ConversationStatus (..),
    startConversation,
    endConversation,
    getConversationStatus,

    -- * Event Handling
    OSEvent (..),
    subscribeToEvents,
    unsubscribeFromEvents,

    -- * Utility Functions
    defaultInterfaceConfig,
    InterfaceMode (..),

    -- * Re-exports from Compat.Runtime (deprecated)
    OS (..),
) where

import Control.Concurrent (ThreadId)
import Control.Concurrent.Async (Async)
import Control.Concurrent.STM (TQueue, TVar, readTVarIO)
import Data.Map.Strict (Map)
import qualified Data.Map.Strict as Map
import Data.Text (Text)
import Data.Time (UTCTime)

import System.Agents.Base (AgentId, ConversationId)
import System.Agents.OS.Compat.Runtime (OS (..))
import System.Agents.OS.Events (OSEvent (..))
import System.Agents.Session.Base (Session, SessionProgress)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Interface Configuration
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-- | Operating mode for the interface.
data InterfaceMode
    = -- | Interactive TUI mode with multi-agent support
      ModeTUI
    | -- | Batch OneShot mode with single agent
      ModeOneShot
    deriving (Show, Eq)

-- | Configuration for initializing an interface.
data InterfaceConfig = InterfaceConfig
    { ifcMode :: InterfaceMode
    -- ^ Operating mode (TUI interactive or OneShot batch)
    , ifcMaxAgents :: Int
    -- ^ Maximum number of concurrent agents (0 for unlimited)
    , ifcEnableEvents :: Bool
    -- ^ Whether to enable event subscription
    , ifcSessionStorePath :: Maybe FilePath
    -- ^ Optional path for session persistence
    }
    deriving (Show, Eq)

-- | Default interface configuration.
defaultInterfaceConfig :: InterfaceConfig
defaultInterfaceConfig =
    InterfaceConfig
        { ifcMode = ModeOneShot
        , ifcMaxAgents = 10
        , ifcEnableEvents = True
        , ifcSessionStorePath = Nothing
        }

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Interface Handle
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

{- | Handle to an initialized OS interface.

The interface handle maintains the connection to the OS and tracks
all active agents and conversations. It uses a TVar for the shutdown
flag to allow atomic updates.
-}
data InterfaceHandle = InterfaceHandle
    { ihOS :: OS
    -- ^ The underlying OS instance
    , ihConfig :: InterfaceConfig
    -- ^ Configuration used to initialize
    , ihEventSub :: TQueue OSEvent
    -- ^ Event queue for subscribers
    , ihShutdown :: TVar Bool
    -- ^ Shutdown flag for graceful termination
    , ihAgents :: TVar (Map AgentId AgentHandle)
    -- ^ Active agents
    , ihConversations :: TVar (Map ConversationId ConversationHandle)
    -- ^ Active conversations
    }

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- OS Interface Typeclass
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-- | Typeclass for OS interface operations.
class OSInterface m where
    -- | Initialize the interface with an OS instance.
    initInterface :: OS -> InterfaceConfig -> m InterfaceHandle

    -- | Run the interface (blocking).
    runInterface :: InterfaceHandle -> m ()

    -- | Shutdown the interface gracefully.
    shutdownInterface :: InterfaceHandle -> m ()

-- | Concrete IO implementation of OSInterface.
instance OSInterface IO where
    initInterface _os _config = do
        error "initInterface: Not yet implemented - use OS-native interface"

    runInterface _handle = do
        error "runInterface: Not yet implemented - use OS-native interface"

    shutdownInterface _handle = do
        error "shutdownInterface: Not yet implemented - use OS-native interface"

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Agent Handle
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-- | Agent status.
data AgentStatus
    = AgentStatus_Idle
    | AgentStatus_Running
    | AgentStatus_Paused
    | AgentStatus_Error Text
    | AgentStatus_Shutdown
    deriving (Show, Eq)

-- | Handle to an agent within the interface.
data AgentHandle = AgentHandle
    { ahAgentId :: AgentId
    -- ^ Unique agent identifier
    , ahThreadId :: Maybe ThreadId
    -- ^ Thread ID if agent is running
    , ahStatus :: AgentStatus
    -- ^ Current agent status
    , ahCreatedAt :: UTCTime
    -- ^ Creation timestamp
    }

-- | Manual Show instance for AgentHandle (Async doesn't have Show).
instance Show AgentHandle where
    show ah =
        "AgentHandle {ahAgentId = "
            ++ show ah.ahAgentId
            ++ ", ahThreadId = "
            ++ show ah.ahThreadId
            ++ ", ahStatus = "
            ++ show ah.ahStatus
            ++ ", ahCreatedAt = "
            ++ show ah.ahCreatedAt
            ++ "}"

-- | Create a new agent in the interface.
createAgent :: InterfaceHandle -> Text -> IO AgentHandle
createAgent _handle _agentName = do
    -- Placeholder implementation
    error "createAgent: Not yet implemented - use OS-native interface"

-- | Destroy an agent and clean up resources.
destroyAgent :: InterfaceHandle -> AgentId -> IO ()
destroyAgent _handle _agentId = do
    -- Placeholder implementation
    error "destroyAgent: Not yet implemented - use OS-native interface"

-- | Send a message to an agent.
sendMessage :: InterfaceHandle -> AgentId -> Text -> IO ()
sendMessage _handle _agentId _message = do
    -- Placeholder implementation
    error "sendMessage: Not yet implemented - use OS-native interface"

-- | Receive a message from an agent (blocking).
receiveMessage :: InterfaceHandle -> AgentId -> IO Text
receiveMessage _handle _agentId = do
    -- Placeholder implementation
    error "receiveMessage: Not yet implemented - use OS-native interface"

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Conversation Handle
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-- | Conversation status.
data ConversationStatus
    = ConversationStatus_Pending
    | ConversationStatus_Active
    | ConversationStatus_WaitingInput
    | ConversationStatus_Completed
    | ConversationStatus_Failed Text
    deriving (Show, Eq)

-- | Handle to a conversation within the interface.
data ConversationHandle = ConversationHandle
    { chConversationId :: ConversationId
    -- ^ Unique conversation identifier
    , chAgentId :: AgentId
    -- ^ Agent participating in this conversation
    , chSession :: Maybe Session
    -- ^ Current session state
    , chAsync :: Maybe (Async ())
    -- ^ Async handle for running conversation
    , chStatus :: ConversationStatus
    -- ^ Current conversation status
    }

-- | Manual Show instance for ConversationHandle (Async doesn't have Show).
instance Show ConversationHandle where
    show ch =
        "ConversationHandle {chConversationId = "
            ++ show ch.chConversationId
            ++ ", chAgentId = "
            ++ show ch.chAgentId
            ++ ", chSession = "
            ++ show ch.chSession
            ++ ", chAsync = <Async>, chStatus = "
            ++ show ch.chStatus
            ++ "}"

-- | Start a new conversation with an agent.
startConversation :: InterfaceHandle -> AgentId -> Text -> IO ConversationHandle
startConversation _handle _agentId _initialMessage = do
    -- Placeholder implementation
    error "startConversation: Not yet implemented - use OS-native interface"

-- | End a conversation.
endConversation :: InterfaceHandle -> ConversationId -> IO ()
endConversation _handle _convId = do
    -- Placeholder implementation
    error "endConversation: Not yet implemented - use OS-native interface"

-- | Get the current status of a conversation.
getConversationStatus :: InterfaceHandle -> ConversationId -> IO ConversationStatus
getConversationStatus handle convId = do
    convs <- readTVarIO (ihConversations handle)
    case Map.lookup convId convs of
        Just conv -> pure (chStatus conv)
        Nothing -> pure (ConversationStatus_Failed "Conversation not found")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Event Handling
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-- | Subscribe to OS events.
subscribeToEvents :: InterfaceHandle -> IO (TQueue OSEvent)
subscribeToEvents handle = pure (ihEventSub handle)

-- | Unsubscribe from OS events.
unsubscribeFromEvents :: InterfaceHandle -> IO ()
unsubscribeFromEvents _handle = do
    -- In a full implementation, this would remove the subscriber
    pure ()

----
{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedRecordDot #-}
{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}

{- | OS Event types for subcall visibility and TUI integration.

This module provides event types that are used for communication between
the OS layer and the TUI, particularly for tracking subcall lifecycle
(started, progress, completed, failed).

By placing these types in a separate module, we avoid circular dependencies
between OS.Interfaces, Session.Base, and Tools.Context.
-}
module System.Agents.OS.Events (
    -- * OS Event Types
    OSEvent (..),
) where

import Control.Concurrent.STM (TQueue)
import Data.Text (Text)

import System.Agents.Base (AgentId, ConversationId)
import System.Agents.Session.Base (Session, SessionProgress)

-- | Events that can be emitted by the OS.
data OSEvent
    = OSEvent_AgentStarted AgentId
    | OSEvent_AgentStopped AgentId
    | OSEvent_ConversationStarted ConversationId AgentId
    | OSEvent_ConversationProgress ConversationId SessionProgress
    | OSEvent_ConversationCompleted ConversationId
    | OSEvent_ConversationFailed ConversationId Text
    | OSEvent_ToolCalled AgentId Text Value
    | OSEvent_ToolCompleted AgentId Text Value
    | OSEvent_Error Text
    | OSEvent_Shutdown
    | -- ** Subcall Events for TUI visibility
      -- | Emitted when a subcall (helper agent invocation) starts
      OSEvent_SubcallStarted
        { subcallParentConversationId :: ConversationId
        -- ^ The parent conversation that initiated the subcall
        , subcallConversationId :: ConversationId
        -- ^ The new conversation ID for the subcall
        , subcallAgentSlug :: Text
        -- ^ The slug of the agent being called
        , subcallDepth :: Int
        -- ^ The recursion depth of this subcall (0 = root)
        }
    | -- | Emitted when a subcall makes progress
      OSEvent_SubcallProgress
        { subcallProgressConversationId :: ConversationId
        -- ^ The subcall conversation ID
        , subcallProgressSession :: Session
        -- ^ The current session state
        }
    | -- | Emitted when a subcall completes successfully
      OSEvent_SubcallCompleted
        { subcallCompletedConversationId :: ConversationId
        -- ^ The subcall conversation ID
        , subcallCompletedResult :: Text
        -- ^ The result/response text from the subcall
        }
    | -- | Emitted when a subcall fails
      OSEvent_SubcallFailed
        { subcallFailedConversationId :: ConversationId
        -- ^ The subcall conversation ID
        , subcallFailedError :: Text
        -- ^ The error message
        }
    deriving (Show)

33	import qualified System.Agents.Base as Base

35	import qualified System.Agents.HttpClient as HttpClient
36	import qualified System.Agents.LLMs.OpenAI as OpenAI

You are an expert Haskell and Bash programmer specialized in writing clean, idiomatic, and efficient Haskell code.
When writing Bash code:
- Avoid bash-specific logic but bash-ism are fine.
- Document required shell tools at the top of the bash script.
When writing Haskell code:
- Use modern Haskell practices (base >= 4.14)
- Prefer pure functions and explicit type signatures
- Use standard libraries when appropriate
- Add clear comments for complex logic
- Follow consistent formatting
Use list-files to explore the project structure, read-file to understand existing code, write-file to create or modify files, and check-code to verify your work.
Always compile code before considering the job done.
Your output format may be stored as commit message: hence the first line of your last message should be a summary line.
You have access to a tool which is a copy of yourself named io_prompt_agent_oneshot, use it when a task is well-scoped in a way that it would save tokens in your context window and not interfere with your work.

content.content:
275	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
276	
277	-- | Events that can be emitted by the OS.
278	data OSEvent
279	    = OSEvent_AgentStarted AgentId
280	    | OSEvent_AgentStopped AgentId
281	    | OSEvent_ConversationStarted ConversationId AgentId
282	    | OSEvent_ConversationProgress ConversationId SessionProgress
283	    | OSEvent_ConversationCompleted ConversationId
284	    | OSEvent_ConversationFailed ConversationId Text
285	    | OSEvent_ToolCalled AgentId Text Value
286	    | OSEvent_ToolCompleted AgentId Text Value
287	    | OSEvent_Error Text
288	    | OSEvent_Shutdown
289	    | -- ** Subcall Events for TUI visibility
290	      -- | Emitted when a subcall (helper agent invocation) starts
291	      OSEvent_SubcallStarted
292	        { subcallParentConversationId :: ConversationId
293	        -- ^ The parent conversation that initiated the subcall
294	        , subcallConversationId :: ConversationId
295	        -- ^ The new conversation ID for the subcall
296	        , subcallAgentSlug :: Text
297	        -- ^ The slug of the agent being called
298	        , subcallDepth :: Int
299	        -- ^ The recursion depth of this subcall (0 = root)
300	        }
301	    | -- | Emitted when a subcall makes progress
302	      OSEvent_SubcallProgress
303	        { subcallProgressConversationId :: ConversationId
304	        -- ^ The subcall conversation ID
305	        , subcallProgressSession :: Session
306	        -- ^ The current session state
307	        }
308	    | -- | Emitted when a subcall completes successfully
309	      OSEvent_SubcallCompleted
310	        { subcallCompletedConversationId :: ConversationId
311	        -- ^ The subcall conversation ID
312	        , subcallCompletedResult :: Text
313	        -- ^ The result/response text from the subcall
314	        }
315	    | -- | Emitted when a subcall fails
316	      OSEvent_SubcallFailed
317	        { subcallFailedConversationId :: ConversationId
318	        -- ^ The subcall conversation ID
319	        , subcallFailedError :: Text
320	        -- ^ The error message
321	        }
322	    deriving (Show)
323	
324	-- | Subscribe to OS events.
325	subscribeToEvents :: InterfaceHandle -> IO (TQueue OSEvent)
326	subscribeToEvents handle = pure (ihEventSub handle)
327	
328	-- | Unsubscribe from OS events.
329	unsubscribeFromEvents :: InterfaceHandle -> IO ()
330	unsubscribeFromEvents _handle = do

201	-- | Send a message to an agent.
202	sendMessage :: InterfaceHandle -> AgentId -> Text -> IO ()
203	sendMessage _handle _agentId _message = do
204	    -- Placeholder implementation
205	    error "sendMessage: Not yet implemented - use OS-native interface"
206	
207	-- | Receive a message from an agent (blocking).
208	receiveMessage :: InterfaceHandle -> AgentId -> IO Text
209	receiveMessage _handle _agentId = do

211	    error "receiveMessage: Not yet implemented - use OS-native interface"
212	
213	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
214	-- Conversation Handle
215	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
216	
217	-- | Conversation status.
218	data ConversationStatus
219	    = ConversationStatus_Pending
220	    | ConversationStatus_Active
221	    | ConversationStatus_WaitingInput
222	    | ConversationStatus_Completed
223	    | ConversationStatus_Failed Text
224	    deriving (Show, Eq)
225	
226	-- | Handle to a conversation within the interface.
227	data ConversationHandle = ConversationHandle
228	    { chConversationId :: ConversationId
229	    -- ^ Unique conversation identifier
230	    , chAgentId :: AgentId
231	    -- ^ Agent participating in this conversation
232	    , chSession :: Maybe Session
233	    -- ^ Current session state
234	    , chAsync :: Maybe (Async ())
235	    -- ^ Async handle for running conversation
236	    , chStatus :: ConversationStatus
237	    -- ^ Current conversation status
238	    }
239	
240	-- | Manual Show instance for ConversationHandle (Async doesn't have Show).
241	instance Show ConversationHandle where
242	    show ch =
243	        "ConversationHandle {chConversationId = "
244	            ++ show ch.chConversationId
245	            ++ ", chAgentId = "
246	            ++ show ch.chAgentId
247	            ++ ", chSession = "
248	            ++ show ch.chSession
249	            ++ ", chAsync = <Async>, chStatus = "
250	            ++ show ch.chStatus
251	            ++ "}"
252	
253	-- | Start a new conversation with an agent.
254	startConversation :: InterfaceHandle -> AgentId -> Text -> IO ConversationHandle
255	startConversation _handle _agentId _initialMessage = do
256	    -- Placeholder implementation
257	    error "startConversation: Not yet implemented - use OS-native interface"
258	
259	-- | End a conversation.
260	endConversation :: InterfaceHandle -> ConversationId -> IO ()
261	endConversation _handle _convId = do
262	    -- Placeholder implementation
263	    error "endConversation: Not yet implemented - use OS-native interface"
264	
265	-- | Get the current status of a conversation.
266	getConversationStatus :: InterfaceHandle -> ConversationId -> IO ConversationStatus
267	getConversationStatus handle convId = do
268	    convs <- readTVarIO (ihConversations handle)
269	    case Map.lookup convId convs of
270	        Just conv -> pure (chStatus conv)

{"capability": "write-file-range", "path": "src/System/Agents/OS/Interfaces.hs", "ranges": "1-50", "content": "1	{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedRecordDot #-}
2	{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}
3	
4	{- | Common interface abstraction for TUI and OneShot interfaces.
5	
6	This module provides a unified interface that both TUI and OneShot can use
7	to interact with the OS. The interface uses OS-native structures.
8	-}
9	module System.Agents.OS.Interfaces (
10	    -- * Interface Handle
10	    InterfaceHandle (..),
11	    InterfaceConfig (..),
12	
12	    -- * Interface Operations (via typeclass)
13	    OSInterface (..),
13	
14	    -- * Agent Operations
15	    AgentHandle (..),
16	    AgentStatus (..),
17	    createAgent,
18	    destroyAgent,
19	    sendMessage,
20	    receiveMessage,
21	
21	    -- * Conversation Operations
22	    ConversationHandle (..),
23	    ConversationStatus (..),
24	    startConversation,
25	    endConversation,
26	    getConversationStatus,
27	
28	    -- * Event Handling
28	    OSEvent (..),
29	    subscribeToEvents,
30	    unsubscribeFromEvents,
30	
31	    -- * Utility Functions
31	    defaultInterfaceConfig,
32	    InterfaceMode (..),
32	
33	    -- * Re-exports from Compat.Runtime (deprecated)
34	    OS (..),
35	) where
35	
36	import Control.Concurrent (ThreadId)
37	import Control.Concurrent.Async (Async)
38	import Control.Concurrent.STM (TQueue, TVar, readTVarIO)
39	import Data.Aeson (Value)
40	import Data.Map.Strict (Map)
41	import qualified Data.Map.Strict as Map
42	import Data.Text (Text)
43	import Data.Time (UTCTime)
44	
45	import System.Agents.Base (AgentId, ConversationId)
46	import System.Agents.OS.Compat.Runtime (OS (..))
47	import System.Agents.OS.Events (OSEvent (..))
48	import System.Agents.Session.Base (Session, SessionProgress)"}

contents.userPrompt:
You are an expert Haskell and Bash programmer specialized in writing clean, idiomatic, and efficient Haskell code.
When writing Bash code:
- Avoid bash-specific logic but bash-ism are fine.
- Document required shell tools at the top of the bash script.
When writing Haskell code:
- Use modern Haskell practices (base >= 4.14)
- Prefer pure functions and explicit type signatures
- Use standard libraries when appropriate
- Add clear comments for complex logic
- Follow consistent formatting
Use list-files to explore the project structure, read-file to understand existing code, write-file to create or modify files, and check-code to verify your work.
Always compile code before considering the job done.
Your output format may be stored as commit message: hence the first line of your last message should be a summary line.
You have access to a tool which is a copy of yourself named io_prompt_agent_oneshot, use it when a task is well-scoped in a way that it would save tokens in your context window and not interfere with your work.

function.arguments:
{"filepath": "src/System/Agents/OS/Events.hs", "content": "{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedRecordDot #-}
{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}

{- | OS Event types for subcall visibility and TUI integration.

This module provides event types that are used for communication between
the OS layer and the TUI, particularly for tracking subcall lifecycle
(started, progress, completed, failed).

By placing these types in a separate module, we avoid circular dependencies
between OS.Interfaces, Session.Base, and Tools.Context.
-}
module System.Agents.OS.Events (
    -- * OS Event Types
    OSEvent (..),
) where

import Control.Concurrent.STM (TQueue)
import Data.Text (Text)

import System.Agents.Base (AgentId, ConversationId)
import System.Agents.Session.Base (Session, SessionProgress)

-- | Events that can be emitted by the OS.
data OSEvent
    = OSEvent_AgentStarted AgentId
    | OSEvent_AgentStopped AgentId
    | OSEvent_ConversationStarted ConversationId AgentId
    | OSEvent_ConversationProgress ConversationId SessionProgress
    | OSEvent_ConversationCompleted ConversationId
    | OSEvent_ConversationFailed ConversationId Text
    | OSEvent_ToolCalled AgentId Text Value
    | OSEvent_ToolCompleted AgentId Text Value
    | OSEvent_Error Text
    | OSEvent_Shutdown
    | -- ** Subcall Events for TUI visibility
      -- | Emitted when a subcall (helper agent invocation) starts
      OSEvent_SubcallStarted
        { subcallParentConversationId :: ConversationId
        -- ^ The parent conversation that initiated the subcall
        , subcallConversationId :: ConversationId
        -- ^ The new conversation ID for the subcall
        , subcallAgentSlug :: Text
        -- ^ The slug of the agent being called
        , subcallDepth :: Int
        -- ^ The recursion depth of this subcall (0 = root)
        }
    | -- | Emitted when a subcall makes progress
      OSEvent_SubcallProgress
        { subcallProgressConversationId :: ConversationId
        -- ^ The subcall conversation ID
        , subcallProgressSession :: Session
        -- ^ The current session state
        }
    | -- | Emitted when a subcall completes successfully
      OSEvent_SubcallCompleted
        { subcallCompletedConversationId :: ConversationId
        -- ^ The subcall conversation ID
        , subcallCompletedResult :: Text
        -- ^ The result/response text from the subcall
        }
    | -- | Emitted when a subcall fails
      OSEvent_SubcallFailed
        { subcallFailedConversationId :: ConversationId
        -- ^ The subcall conversation ID
        , subcallFailedError :: Text
        -- ^ The error message
        }
    deriving (Show)
", "check_action": "none"}

contents.userPrompt:
You are an expert Haskell and Bash programmer specialized in writing clean, idiomatic, and efficient Haskell code.
When writing Bash code:
- Avoid bash-specific logic but bash-ism are fine.
- Document required shell tools at the top of the bash script.
When writing Haskell code:
- Use modern Haskell practices (base >= 4.14)
- Prefer pure functions and explicit type signatures
- Use standard libraries when appropriate
- Add clear comments for complex logic
- Follow consistent formatting
Use list-files to explore the project structure, read-file to understand existing code, write-file to create or modify files, and check-code to verify your work.
Always compile code before considering the job done.
Your output format may be stored as commit message: hence the first line of your last message should be a summary line.
You have access to a tool which is a copy of yourself named io_prompt_agent_oneshot, use it when a task is well-scoped in a way that it would save tokens in your context window and not interfere with your work.

function.arguments:
{"capability": "write-file-range", "path": "src/System/Agents/TUI/Render.hs", "ranges": "375-410", "content": "375	-- | Render a single conversation item.
376	render_conversationItem :: TuiState -> Bool -> Conversation -> Widget N
377	render_conversationItem st isSelected conv =
378	    let indicator = case conversationStatus conv of
379	            ConversationStatus_Active -> "⟳ "
380	            ConversationStatus_WaitingForInput ->
381	                if isUnread then "● " else "  "
382	            ConversationStatus_Paused -> "⏸ "
383	        -- Add indentation for subcalls based on depth
384	        indent = Text.replicate (conversationSubcallDepth conv * 2) " "
385	        baseText = indicator <> indent <> Text.take 18 (conversationName conv)
386	        -- Add turn count next to conversation name
387	        turnCount = case conversationSession conv of
388	            Nothing -> 0
389	            Just session -> length session.turns
390	        turnSuffix = if turnCount > 0 then " (" <> Text.pack (show turnCount) <> ")" else ""
391	        -- Add attachment count if any
392	        attachmentCount = getAttachmentCount st conv
393	        attachmentSuffix = if attachmentCount > 0 then " [📎" <> Text.pack (show attachmentCount) <> "]" else ""
394	        -- Add queued message count if any
395	        queueCount = getQueuedMessageCount st conv
396	        queueSuffix = if queueCount > 0 then " [" <> Text.pack (show queueCount) <> " queued]" else ""
397	        fullText = baseText <> turnSuffix <> attachmentSuffix <> queueSuffix
397	        -- Choose widget style based on subcall status and selection
398	        widget = case conversationStatus conv of
399	            ConversationStatus_Paused ->
399	                withAttr pausedAttr $ txt $ " " <> fullText
400	            _ -> 
401	                if conversationIsSubcall conv
402	                    then withAttr (if isSelected then subcallSelectedAttr else subcallAttr) $ txt $ " " <> fullText
403	                    else txt $ " " <> fullText
404	     in widget
405	  where
406	    isUnread = Set.member (conversationId conv) (st ^. tuiUI . unreadConversations)
407	
408	-- | Get the number of queued messages for a conversation.
409	getQueuedMessageCount :: TuiState -> Conversation -> Int
410	getQueuedMessageCount st conv =
411	    let buffered = st ^. tuiUI . uiBufferedMessages
412	     in case Map.lookup (conversationId conv) buffered of
413	            Nothing -> 0
414	            Just msgs -> length msgs"}
function.name: developer_dev_developer_tools

{"capability": "write-file-range", "path": "src/System/Agents/TUI/Render.hs", "ranges": "165-180", "content": "165	-- | Attribute for dialog overlays.
166	dialogAttr :: AttrName
167	dialogAttr = attrName "dialog"
168	
169	-- | Attribute for subcall conversations (dimmed).
170	subcallAttr :: AttrName
171	subcallAttr = attrName "subcall"
172	
173	-- | Attribute for selected subcall conversations.
174	subcallSelectedAttr :: AttrName
175	subcallSelectedAttr = attrName "subcallSelected"
176	
177	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
178	-- Main Draw Function
178	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
179	
180	-- | Main application draw function.
181	tui_appDraw :: TuiState -> [Widget N]
182	tui_appDraw st =
183	    case st ^. tuiUI . attachmentDialogState of
184	        AttachmentDialogPathInput -> [renderFilePathDialog st, render_ui st]
185	        AttachmentDialogFileBrowser -> [renderFileBrowserDialog st, render_ui st]
186	        AttachmentDialogClosed -> [render_ui st]"}

950	                    , if null llmTurn.llmToolCalls
951	                        then emptyWidget
952	                        else
953	                            vBox
954	                                [ txt $ "  [tool calls: " <> Text.pack (show (length llmTurn.llmToolCalls)) <> "]"
955	                                , render_usage mUsage
956	                                , txt " "

959	
960	-- | Render usage info for a turn (tokens if available, else bytes).
961	render_usage :: Maybe StepByteUsage -> Widget N
962	render_usage Nothing = emptyWidget
963	render_usage (Just usage) =
964	    case stepTokenUsage usage of
965	        Just tokenUsage ->
966	            withAttr tokenUsageAttr $ txt $ "[" <> formatTokenUsage tokenUsage <> "]"
967	        Nothing ->
968	            withAttr byteUsageAttr $ txt $ "[" <> formatByteBreakdown usage <> "]"
969	
970	-- | Format token usage for display.
971	formatTokenUsage :: TokenUsage -> Text
972	formatTokenUsage usage =
973	    let parts =
974	            concat
975	                [ ["in: " <> formatTokenCount (tokenPromptTokens usage)]
976	                , ["out: " <> formatTokenCount (tokenCompletionTokens usage)]
977	                , case tokenCachedTokens usage of
978	                    Just n | n > 0 -> ["cached: " <> formatTokenCount n]
979	                    _ -> []
980	                , case tokenThinkingTokens usage of
981	                    Just n | n > 0 -> ["think: " <> formatTokenCount n]
982	                    _ -> []
983	                ]
984	     in Text.intercalate ", " parts <> " | total: " <> formatTokenCount (tokenTotalTokens usage)
985	
986	{- | Format byte usage breakdown for display.
987	TODO: display tools and tool-responses explicitly
988	-}
989	formatByteBreakdown :: StepByteUsage -> Text
990	formatByteBreakdown usage =
991	    let parts =
992	            concat
993	                [ if stepInputBytes usage > 0 then ["in: " <> formatBytes (stepInputBytes usage)] else []
994	                , if stepOutputBytes usage > 0 then ["out: " <> formatBytes (stepOutputBytes usage)] else []
995	                , if stepReasoningBytes usage > 0 then ["reason: " <> formatBytes (stepReasoningBytes usage)] else []
996	                , if stepToolBytes usage > 0 then ["tool: " <> formatBytes (stepToolBytes usage)] else []
997	                ]
998	     in if null parts
999	            then "total: " <> formatBytes (stepTotalBytes usage)
1000	            else Text.intercalate ", " parts
1001	
1002	-- | Helper to extract text from SystemPrompt.
1003	getSystemPromptText :: SystemPrompt -> Text
1004	getSystemPromptText (SystemPrompt txt0) = txt0
1005	
1006	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1007	-- Helper Functions
1008	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1009	
1010	-- | Create a border that shows focus.
1011	borderWithFocus :: TuiState -> WidgetName -> Text -> Widget n -> Widget n
1012	borderWithFocus st widgetName labelText content =
1013	    let labelWidget =
1014	            if focusGetCurrent (st ^. tuiUI . uiFocusRing) == Just widgetName
1015	                then withAttr focusedAttr (txt labelText)
1016	                else txt labelText
1017	     in borderWithLabel labelWidget content
1018	
1019	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1020	-- Attribute Map
1021	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1022	
1023	-- | Attribute map for styling.
1024	tui_appAttrMap :: TuiState -> AttrMap
1025	tui_appAttrMap _ =
1026	    attrMap
1027	        Vty.defAttr
1028	        [ (focusedAttr, BrickUtil.bg Vty.blue)
1029	        , (listSelectedAttr, Vty.defAttr `Vty.withForeColor` Vty.blue)
1030	        , (userMessageAttr, BrickUtil.fg Vty.green)
1031	        , (llmMessageAttr, BrickUtil.fg Vty.cyan)
1032	        , (thinkingAttr, BrickUtil.fg Vty.magenta `Vty.withStyle` Vty.italic)
1033	        , (byteUsageAttr, BrickUtil.fg Vty.brightYellow `Vty.withStyle` Vty.dim)
1034	        , (tokenUsageAttr, BrickUtil.fg Vty.brightGreen `Vty.withStyle` Vty.dim)
1035	        , (signalMetricsAttr, BrickUtil.fg Vty.brightCyan `Vty.withStyle` Vty.dim)
1036	        , (attrName "help", BrickUtil.fg Vty.yellow `Vty.withStyle` Vty.dim)
1037	        , (statusInfoAttr, BrickUtil.fg Vty.white `Vty.withStyle` Vty.dim)
1038	        , (statusWarningAttr, BrickUtil.fg Vty.yellow)
1039	        , (statusErrorAttr, BrickUtil.fg Vty.red `Vty.withStyle` Vty.bold)
1040	        , (pausedAttr, BrickUtil.fg Vty.yellow `Vty.withStyle` Vty.bold)

content.content:
700	            Nothing -> []
701	            Just msgs -> reverse msgs -- Reverse to show oldest first
702	
703	-- | Render the session history view.
704	render_sessionView :: TuiState -> Widget N
705	render_sessionView st =
706	    content
707	  where
708	    content =
709	        case listSelectedElement (st ^. tuiUI . sessionList) of
710	            Nothing -> do
711	                txt "No session selected"
712	            Just (_, session) ->
713	                let mNavState = st ^. tuiUI . turnNavigation
714	                 in render_session st SessionViewWidget (Just session) (st ^. tuiUI . ongoingConversations) mNavState
715	
716	-- | Render a session's turns.
717	render_session :: TuiState -> WidgetName -> Maybe Session -> Set ConversationId -> Maybe TurnNavigationState -> Widget N
718	render_session _ _ Nothing _ _ =
719	    vBox $ [txt "session not started yet"]
720	render_session st w (Just session) _ongoingConvs mNavState =
721	    case mNavState of
722	        Nothing ->
723	            -- Normal mode: render as before
724	            borderWithFocus st w "Session" $
725	                viewport w Both $
726	                    vBox $
727	                        [render_session_usage session]
728	                            ++ map render_turn (Prelude.reverse (zip [(0 :: Int) ..] $ Prelude.reverse session.turns))
729	        Just navState ->
730	            -- Navigation mode: render with selection highlight
731	            render_turn_navigation session navState
732	
733	-- | Render session in turn navigation mode.
734	render_turn_navigation :: Session -> TurnNavigationState -> Widget N
735	render_turn_navigation session navState =
736	    let selectedIdx = navState ^. navSelectedTurnIndex
737	        totalTurns = navState ^. navTotalTurns
738	        headerText = "Turn Navigation (" <> Text.pack (show (selectedIdx + 1)) <> "/" <> Text.pack (show totalTurns) <> ") [Enter:exit F:fork]"
739	        turnsWithIndices = zip [0 ..] session.turns -- Maintain chronological order
740	        shownTurns = drop selectedIdx turnsWithIndices
741	     in borderWithLabel (txt headerText) $
742	            viewport TurnNavigationWidget Both $
743	                vBox $
744	                    [ txt "Up/Down: navigate  Enter: exit  F: fork from here"
745	                    , txt ""
746	                    ]
747	                        ++ map (render_navigable_turn selectedIdx) shownTurns
748	
749	-- | Render a single turn with selection indicator.
750	render_navigable_turn :: Int -> (Int, Turn) -> Widget N
751	render_navigable_turn selectedIdx (idx, turn) =
752	    let isSelected = idx == selectedIdx
753	        selectionMarker = if isSelected then "▶ " else "  "
754	        turnWidget = render_turn (idx, turn)
755	     in if isSelected
756	            then
757	                withAttr selectedTurnAttr $
758	                    hBox [txt selectionMarker, turnWidget]
759	            else hBox [txt selectionMarker, turnWidget]
760	
761	-- | Render queued messages for a conversation (legacy inline display).
762	render_queued_messages :: [Text] -> Widget N
763	render_queued_messages [] = emptyWidget
764	render_queued_messages msgs =
765	    withAttr queuedMessageAttr $
766	        vBox $
767	            [txt "Queued messages:"]
768	                ++ map (\m -> txt $ "  ▶ " <> Text.take 60 m) msgs
769	                ++ [txt ""]
770	
771	-- | Render total session usage (tokens if available, else bytes), turn count, and signal metrics.
772	render_session_usage :: Session -> Widget N
773	render_session_usage session =
774	    let turnCount = length session.turns
775	        -- Try to get aggregated token usage from all turns
776	        mTokenStats = aggregateSessionTokenUsage session
777	        -- Calculate trajectory signals
778	        mSignals = Just (calculateTrajectorySignals session)
779	     in if turnCount == 0
780	            then emptyWidget
781	            else
782	                vBox
783	                    [ case mTokenStats of
784	                        Just tokenStats ->
785	                            withAttr tokenUsageAttr $
786	                                txt $
787	                                    "Session total: " <> formatTokenStats tokenStats <> "  (" <> Text.pack (show turnCount) <> " turns)  "
788	                        Nothing ->
789	                            let totalBytes = sessionTotalBytes session
790	                             in if totalBytes == 0
791	                                    then emptyWidget
792	                                    else
793	                                        withAttr byteUsageAttr $
794	                                            txt $
795	                                                "Session total: " <> formatBytes totalBytes <> "  (" <> Text.pack (show turnCount) <> " turns)  "
796	                    , renderSignalSummary mSignals
797	                    ]
798	
799	-- | Render compact signal summary for TUI header.
800	renderSignalSummary :: Maybe TrajectorySignals -> Widget N

content.content:
365	-- | Render a single conversation item.
366	render_conversationItem :: TuiState -> Bool -> Conversation -> Widget N
367	render_conversationItem st _ conv =
368	    let indicator = case conversationStatus conv of
369	            ConversationStatus_Active -> "⟳ "
370	            ConversationStatus_WaitingForInput ->
371	                if isUnread then "● " else "  "
372	            ConversationStatus_Paused -> "⏸ "
373	        baseText = indicator <> Text.take 18 (conversationName conv)
374	        -- Add turn count next to conversation name
375	        turnCount = case conversationSession conv of
376	            Nothing -> 0
377	            Just session -> length session.turns
378	        turnSuffix = if turnCount > 0 then " (" <> Text.pack (show turnCount) <> ")" else ""
379	        -- Add attachment count if any
380	        attachmentCount = getAttachmentCount st conv
381	        attachmentSuffix = if attachmentCount > 0 then " [📎" <> Text.pack (show attachmentCount) <> "]" else ""
382	        -- Add queued message count if any
383	        queueCount = getQueuedMessageCount st conv
384	        queueSuffix = if queueCount > 0 then " [" <> Text.pack (show queueCount) <> " queued]" else ""
385	        fullText = baseText <> turnSuffix <> attachmentSuffix <> queueSuffix
386	        widget = case conversationStatus conv of
387	            ConversationStatus_Paused ->
388	                withAttr pausedAttr $ txt $ " " <> fullText
389	            _ -> txt $ " " <> fullText
390	     in widget
391	  where
392	    isUnread = Set.member (conversationId conv) (st ^. tuiUI . unreadConversations)
393	
394	-- | Get the number of queued messages for a conversation.
395	getQueuedMessageCount :: TuiState -> Conversation -> Int
396	getQueuedMessageCount st conv =
397	    let buffered = st ^. tuiUI . uiBufferedMessages
398	     in case Map.lookup (conversationId conv) buffered of
399	            Nothing -> 0
400	            Just msgs -> length msgs
401	
402	-- | Get the number of attachments for a conversation.
403	getAttachmentCount :: TuiState -> Conversation -> Int
404	getAttachmentCount st conv =
405	    let attachments = st ^. tuiUI . attachedFiles
406	     in case Map.lookup (conversationId conv) attachments of
407	            Nothing -> 0
408	            Just atts -> length atts
409	
410	-- | Render the sessions list.
411	render_sessionList :: TuiState -> Widget N
412	render_sessionList st =
413	    borderWithFocus
414	        st
415	        SessionsListWidget
416	        "Sessions"
417	        (renderList (render_sessionItem st) hasFocus (st ^. tuiUI . sessionList))
418	  where
419	    hasFocus = focusGetCurrent (st ^. tuiUI . uiFocusRing) == Just SessionsListWidget
420	
421	-- | Render a single conversation item.
422	render_sessionItem :: TuiState -> Bool -> Session -> Widget N
423	render_sessionItem _st _ sess =
424	    let
425	        widget = txt $ Text.pack $ " " <> show sess.sessionId
426	     in
427	        widget
428	
429	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
430	-- Agent Info Rendering

313	-- | Render status bar if there's a message.
314	render_statusBar :: Maybe StatusMessage -> Widget N
315	render_statusBar Nothing = emptyWidget
316	render_statusBar (Just msg) =
317	    withAttr (statusAttr msg.statusSeverity) $
318	        txt $
319	            " " <> statusText msg
320	
321	-- | Get the appropriate attribute for a status severity level.
322	statusAttr :: StatusSeverity -> AttrName
323	statusAttr StatusInfo = statusInfoAttr
324	statusAttr StatusWarning = statusWarningAttr
325	statusAttr StatusError = statusErrorAttr

340	    hasFocus = focusGetCurrent (st ^. tuiUI . uiFocusRing) == Just AgentListWidget

342	-- | Render a single agent item.
343	render_agentItem :: Bool -> TuiAgent -> Widget N
344	render_agentItem _ agent =
345	    txt $ " " <> agentSlug0
346	  where

180	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
181	
182	-- | Main layout with tab bar, sidebar, and content area.
183	render_mainLayout :: TuiState -> Widget N
184	render_mainLayout st =
185	    vBox
186	        [ renderTabBar (st ^. tuiUI . currentTab)
187	        , hBox
188	            [ render_sidebar st
189	            , render_contentArea st
190	            ]
191	        , render_statusBar (st ^. tuiUI . statusMessage)
192	        ]
193	
194	-- | Render the tab bar with all tabs, highlighting the active one.
195	renderTabBar :: Tab -> Widget N
196	renderTabBar activeTab =
197	    let tabs = [AgentsTab, ChatsTab, HistoryTab, HelpTab]
198	        renderTab tab =
199	            let tabName = case tab of
200	                    AgentsTab -> " Agents "
201	                    ChatsTab -> " Chats "
202	                    HistoryTab -> " History "
203	                    HelpTab -> " Help "
204	                tabAttr = if tab == activeTab then activeTabAttr else inactiveTabAttr
205	             in withAttr tabAttr $ txt tabName
206	        tabWidgets = map renderTab tabs
207	        separator = withAttr inactiveTabAttr $ txt "│"
208	     in hBorder <=> hBox (intersperse separator tabWidgets)
209	
210	-- | Sidebar with agent and conversation lists.
211	render_sidebar :: TuiState -> Widget N
212	render_sidebar st =
213	    hLimit 30 $
214	        vBox
215	            [ render_agentList st
216	            , render_conversationList st
217	            , render_sessionList st
218	            ]
219	
220	-- | Content area showing content based on the current tab.
221	render_contentArea :: TuiState -> Widget N
222	render_contentArea st =
223	    case st ^. tuiUI . currentTab of
224	        AgentsTab -> renderAgentsTab st
225	        ChatsTab -> renderChatsTab st
226	        HistoryTab -> renderHistoryTab st
227	        HelpTab -> renderHelpTab st
228	
229	-- | Render the Agents tab content.
230	renderAgentsTab :: TuiState -> Widget N
231	renderAgentsTab st =
232	    case focusGetCurrent (st ^. tuiUI . uiFocusRing) of
233	        Just AgentListWidget -> render_agentDetail st
234	        Just AgentInfoWidget -> render_agentDetail st
235	        _ -> render_agentDetail st
236	
237	-- | Render the Chats tab content.
238	renderChatsTab :: TuiState -> Widget N
239	renderChatsTab st =
240	    case focusGetCurrent (st ^. tuiUI . uiFocusRing) of
241	        Just MessageEditorWidget -> render_conversationArea st
242	        Just ConversationListWidget -> render_conversationArea st
243	        Just ConversationViewWidget -> render_conversationArea st
244	        Just AttachmentListWidget -> render_conversationArea st
245	        _ -> render_conversationArea st
246	
247	-- | Render the History tab content (sessions view).
248	renderHistoryTab :: TuiState -> Widget N
249	renderHistoryTab st =
250	    case focusGetCurrent (st ^. tuiUI . uiFocusRing) of

2	{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}
3	
4	-- | Rendering functions for the TUI application.
5	module System.Agents.TUI.Render where
6	
7	import Brick
8	import Brick.Focus (focusGetCurrent)
9	import qualified Brick.Util as BrickUtil
10	import Brick.Widgets.Border (borderWithLabel, hBorder)
11	import Brick.Widgets.Center (center)
12	import Brick.Widgets.Edit (renderEditor)
13	import Brick.Widgets.FileBrowser (
14	    fileBrowserAttr,
15	    fileBrowserCurrentDirectoryAttr,
16	    fileBrowserDirectoryAttr,
17	    fileBrowserRegularFileAttr,
18	    fileBrowserSelectedAttr,
19	    fileBrowserSelectionInfoAttr,
20	    renderFileBrowser,
21	 )
22	import Brick.Widgets.List (listSelectedAttr, listSelectedElement, renderList)
23	import Control.Lens ((^.))
24	import Data.List (intersperse)
25	import qualified Data.Map.Strict as Map
26	import Data.Set (Set)
27	import qualified Data.Set as Set
28	import Data.Text (Text)
29	import qualified Data.Text as Text
30	import qualified Graphics.Vty as Vty
31	
32	import System.Agents.AgentTree (OSAgentNode (..))
33	import System.Agents.Base (Agent (..), ConversationId)
34	import System.Agents.LLMs.OpenAI (TokenUsage (..))
35	import System.Agents.Media.Types (MediaAttachment (..))
36	import System.Agents.Session.Base hiding (Agent)
37	import System.Agents.Session.Signals (
38	    calculateTrajectorySignals,
39	 )
40	import System.Agents.Session.Types (
41	    ExecutionSignals (..),
42	    InteractionSignals (..),
43	    StepByteUsage (..),
44	    TrajectorySignals (..),
45	    sessionTotalBytes,
46	 )
47	import System.Agents.TUI.Types
48	import System.Agents.ToolRegistration (ToolRegistration, declareTool, toolActivation)
49	import System.Agents.ToolSchema (ToolDescription (..), ToolName (..))
50	import System.Agents.Tools.Activation (Activation (..))
51	
52	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
53	-- Attribute Names
54	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
55	
56	-- | Attribute for focused widgets.
57	focusedAttr :: AttrName
58	focusedAttr = attrName "focused"
59	
60	-- | Attribute for user messages.
61	userMessageAttr :: AttrName
62	userMessageAttr = attrName "userMessage"
63	
64	-- | Attribute for LLM messages.
65	llmMessageAttr :: AttrName
66	llmMessageAttr = attrName "llmMessage"
67	
68	-- | Attribute for thinking/reasoning content.
69	thinkingAttr :: AttrName
70	thinkingAttr = attrName "thinking"
71	
72	-- | Attribute for info status messages.
73	statusInfoAttr :: AttrName
74	statusInfoAttr = attrName "statusInfo"
75	
76	-- | Attribute for warning status messages.
77	statusWarningAttr :: AttrName
78	statusWarningAttr = attrName "statusWarning"
79	
80	-- | Attribute for error status messages.
81	statusErrorAttr :: AttrName
82	statusErrorAttr = attrName "statusError"
83	
84	-- | Attribute for byte usage text (dimmed/smaller).
85	byteUsageAttr :: AttrName
86	byteUsageAttr = attrName "byteUsage"
87	
88	-- | Attribute for token usage text.
89	tokenUsageAttr :: AttrName
90	tokenUsageAttr = attrName "tokenUsage"
91	
92	-- | Attribute for signal metrics text.
93	signalMetricsAttr :: AttrName
94	signalMetricsAttr = attrName "signalMetrics"
95	
96	-- | Attribute for paused conversation indicator.
97	pausedAttr :: AttrName
98	pausedAttr = attrName "paused"
99	
100	-- | Attribute for always-on tool activation marker.

{"capability": "write-file-range", "path": "src/System/Agents/TUI/Event.hs", "ranges": "1810-1850", "content": "1813	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1814	-- Subcall Event Handlers
1815	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1816	
1817	{- | Handle subcall started event.
1818	Creates a new subcall conversation in the TUI.
1819	-}
1820	handleSubcallStarted :: ConversationId -> ConversationId -> Text -> Int -> EventM N TuiState ()
1821	handleSubcallStarted parentId subcallId slug depth = do
1822	    -- Find the parent conversation to get the agent
1823	    coreRef <- use tuiCore
1824	    core <- liftIO $ readTVarIO coreRef
1825	    
1825	    case find ((== parentId) . conversationId) (coreConversations core) of
1826	        Just parentConv -> do
1827	            -- Create event channel for the subcall
1828	            subcallChan <- liftIO $ newBChan 100
1829	            
1830	            -- Get the agent from parent conversation
1831	            let agent = conversationAgent parentConv
1832	            
1833	            -- Create the subcall conversation
1834	            let subcallConv = mkSubcallConversation
1835	                    subcallId
1836	                    agent
1837	                    Nothing  -- No thread yet
1838	                    Nothing  -- No session yet
1839	                    ("@" <> slug)
1840	                    subcallChan
1841	                    ConversationStatus_Active
1842	                    (const $ pure ())  -- No-op progress handler for subcalls
1843	                    parentId
1844	                    depth
1845	            
1846	            -- Add to core
1847	            liftIO $ atomically $ modifyTVar coreRef $ \c ->
1848	                c{coreConversations = subcallConv : coreConversations c}
1849	            
1850	            -- Add to UI
1851	            tuiUI . conversationList %= listInsert 0 subcallConv
1852	            
1853	            -- Show status
1854	            showStatus StatusInfo $ "Subcall started: ↳ " <> slug
1855	        Nothing -> do
1856	            -- Parent not found, show warning
1857	            showStatus StatusWarning $ "Subcall started but parent not found: " <> slug
1858	
1859	{- | Handle subcall progress event.
1860	Updates the subcall conversation's session.
1861	-}
1862	handleSubcallProgress :: ConversationId -> Session -> EventM N TuiState ()
1863	handleSubcallProgress subcallId sess = do
1864	    -- Update the conversation session in core
1864	    coreRef <- use tuiCore
1865	    liftIO $ atomically $ modifyTVar coreRef $ \c ->
1866	        c{coreConversations = updateConversationSession subcallId sess (coreConversations c)}
1867	
1868	{- | Handle subcall completed event.
1869	Updates the subcall conversation status.
1870	-}
1871	handleSubcallCompleted :: ConversationId -> Text -> EventM N TuiState ()
1872	handleSubcallCompleted subcallId _result = do
1873	    -- Update conversation status
1874	    updateConversationStatus subcallId ConversationStatus_WaitingForInput
1875	    -- Show status
1875	    showStatus StatusInfo "Subcall completed"
1876	
1877	{- | Handle subcall failed event.
1878	Updates the subcall conversation status and shows error.
1879	-}
1880	handleSubcallFailed :: ConversationId -> Text -> EventM N TuiState ()
1881	handleSubcallFailed subcallId err = do
1882	    -- Update conversation status
1883	    updateConversationStatus subcallId ConversationStatus_WaitingForInput
1884	    -- Show error status
1885	    showStatus StatusError $ "Subcall failed: " <> err
1886	
1887	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1888	-- Help Content Initialization
1889	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1890	
1891	-- | Initialize help content in UIState.
1892	initHelpContent :: UIState -> UIState
1893	initHelpContent uiState = uiState{_helpContent = defaultHelpContent}"}

1851	            tuiUI . conversationList %= listInsert 0 subcallConv
1852	            
1853	            -- Show status
1854	            showStatus StatusInfo $ "Subcall started: ↳ " <> slug
1855	        Nothing -> do
1856	            -- Parent not found, show warning
1857	            showStatus StatusWarning $ "Subcall started but parent not found: " <> slug
1858	
1859	{- | Handle subcall progress event.
1860	Updates the subcall conversation's session.
1861	-}
1862	handleSubcallProgress :: ConversationId -> Session -> EventM N TuiState ()
1863	handleSubcallProgress subcallId sess = do
1864	    -- Update the conversation session in core
1864	    coreRef <- use tuiCore
1865	    liftIO $ atomically $ modifyTVar coreRef $ \c ->
1866	        c{coreConversations = updateConversationSession subcallId sess (coreConversations c)}
1867	
1868	{- | Handle subcall completed event.
1869	Updates the subcall conversation status.
1870	-}
1871	handleSubcallCompleted :: ConversationId -> Text -> EventM N TuiState ()
1872	handleSubcallCompleted subcallId _result = do
1873	    -- Update conversation status
1874	    updateConversationStatus subcallId ConversationStatus_WaitingForInput
1875	    -- Show status
1875	    showStatus StatusInfo "Subcall completed"
1876	
1877	{- | Handle subcall failed event.
1878	Updates the subcall conversation status and shows error.
1879	-}
1880	handleSubcallFailed :: ConversationId -> Text -> EventM N TuiState ()
1881	handleSubcallFailed subcallId err = do
1882	    -- Update conversation status
1883	    updateConversationStatus subcallId ConversationStatus_WaitingForInput
1884	    -- Show error status
1885	    showStatus StatusError $ "Subcall failed: " <> err
1886	
1887	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1888	-- Help Content Initialization
1889	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1890	
1891	-- | Initialize help content in UIState.
1892	initHelpContent :: UIState -> UIState
1893	initHelpContent uiState = uiState{_helpContent = defaultHelpContent}"}
function.name: developer_dev_developer_tools

You are an expert Haskell and Bash programmer specialized in writing clean, idiomatic, and efficient Haskell code.
When writing Bash code:
- Avoid bash-specific logic but bash-ism are fine.
- Document required shell tools at the top of the bash script.
When writing Haskell code:
- Use modern Haskell practices (base >= 4.14)
- Prefer pure functions and explicit type signatures
- Use standard libraries when appropriate
- Add clear comments for complex logic
- Follow consistent formatting
Use list-files to explore the project structure, read-file to understand existing code, write-file to create or modify files, and check-code to verify your work.
Always compile code before considering the job done.
Your output format may be stored as commit message: hence the first line of your last message should be a summary line.
You have access to a tool which is a copy of yourself named io_prompt_agent_oneshot, use it when a task is well-scoped in a way that it would save tokens in your context window and not interfere with your work.

contents.userPrompt:
You are an expert Haskell and Bash programmer specialized in writing clean, idiomatic, and efficient Haskell code.
When writing Bash code:
- Avoid bash-specific logic but bash-ism are fine.
- Document required shell tools at the top of the bash script.
When writing Haskell code:
- Use modern Haskell practices (base >= 4.14)
- Prefer pure functions and explicit type signatures
- Use standard libraries when appropriate
- Add clear comments for complex logic
- Follow consistent formatting
Use list-files to explore the project structure, read-file to understand existing code, write-file to create or modify files, and check-code to verify your work.
Always compile code before considering the job done.
Your output format may be stored as commit message: hence the first line of your last message should be a summary line.
You have access to a tool which is a copy of yourself named io_prompt_agent_oneshot, use it when a task is well-scoped in a way that it would save tokens in your context window and not interfere with your work.

content.content:
1750	
1751	    -- Add to core
1752	    liftIO $ atomically $ modifyTVar coreRef $ \c ->
1753	        c{coreConversations = conv : coreConversations c}
1754	    -- Update UI
1755	    tuiUI . conversationList %= listInsert 0 conv
1756	    tuiUI . conversationList . listSelectedL .= Just 0
1757	    -- Switch to Chats tab and focus the message editor
1758	    switchToChatsAndFocusMessage
1759	
1760	{- | Send a message in the current conversation.
1761	Messages are now buffered if the conversation is being processed by an agent,
1762	allowing users to "interrupt" or provide additional context while the agent
1763	is executing tool calls.
1764	-}
1765	handleSendMessage :: EventM N TuiState ()
1766	handleSendMessage = do
1767	    -- Get message text
1768	    msgLines <- use (tuiUI . messageEditor . to getEditContents)
1769	    let msgText = Text.strip $ Text.unlines msgLines
1770	
1771	    -- Get current attachments
1772	    mConv <- getFocusedConversation
1773	    attachments <- case mConv of
1774	        Just conv -> do
1775	            atts <- use (tuiUI . attachedFiles)
1776	            pure $ Map.findWithDefault [] (conversationId conv) atts
1777	        Nothing -> pure []
1778	
1779	    -- Only send non-empty messages (or messages with attachments)
1780	    when (not (Text.null msgText) || not (null attachments)) $ do
1781	        case mConv of
1782	            Just conv -> do
1783	                -- Get core reference for message buffering
1784	                coreRef <- use tuiCore
1785	                core <- liftIO $ readTVarIO coreRef
1786	
1787	                -- Check if conversation is already being processed
1788	                ongoing <- use (tuiUI . ongoingConversations)
1789	                let isOngoing = Set.member (conversationId conv) ongoing
1790	
1791	                if isOngoing
1792	                    then do
1793	                        -- Buffer the message - agent will pick it up when collecting tool responses
1794	                        liftIO $ addBufferedMessage (conversationId conv) core msgText
1795	                        showStatus StatusInfo "Message buffered - will be sent with tool responses"
1796	                    else do
1797	                        -- Conversation is waiting for input - send directly via channel
1798	                        liftIO $ writeBChan conv.conversationChan (Just $ UserQuery msgText attachments)
1799	                        -- Mark as ongoing since we're starting agent processing
1800	                        tuiUI . ongoingConversations %= Set.insert (conversationId conv)
1801	
1802	                -- Clear the editor and attachments
1803	                tuiUI . messageEditor . editContentsL .= TextZipper.textZipper [] Nothing
1804	                tuiUI . attachedFiles %= Map.delete (conversationId conv)
1805	                tuiUI . selectedAttachmentIndex .= Nothing
1806	            Nothing -> pure ()
1807	
1808	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1809	-- Help Content Initialization
1810	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1811	
1812	-- | Initialize help content in UIState.
1813	initHelpContent :: UIState -> UIState
1814	initHelpContent uiState = uiState{_helpContent = defaultHelpContent}

content.content:
1680	runConversation tracer baseTuiAgent session = do
1681	    -- Get session configuration (includes API keys)
1682	    config <- use sessionConfig
1683	
1684	    -- Generate conversation ID
1685	    convId <- liftIO $ newConversationId
1686	
1687	    outChan <- use eventChan
1688	    inChan <- liftIO $ newBChan 100
1689	
1690	    -- Build the progress callback
1691	    let notifyProgress = buildOnProgress convId outChan
1692	
1693	    let node = tuiNode baseTuiAgent
1694	
1695	    -- Create the agent with the progress callback
1696	    -- Use the agent's OSAgentNode through the TuiAgent
1697	    -- Pass API keys from the session config
1698	    agent0 <- liftIO $ nodeToAgent config.sessionStore Nothing convId (contramap OneShotTrace tracer) config.sessionApiKeys node
1699	    agent1 <- liftIO $ agentEvaluateActiveTools (contramap (OneShotTrace . mapProgressiveDisclosureTrace) tracer) (osNodeTools node) agent0
1700	
1701	    -- Get reference to core state for pause checking and message buffering
1702	    coreRef <- use tuiCore
1703	
1704	    let notifyNeedInput = writeBChan outChan (AppEvent_AgentNeedsInput convId)
1705	    let a =
1706	            agent1
1707	                { step = \sess -> do
1708	                    -- Check if conversation is paused and block until unpaused
1709	                    let waitIfPaused = do
1710	                            core <- readTVarIO coreRef
1711	                            when (isConversationPaused convId core) $ do
1712	                                threadDelay 200000 -- Check every 200ms
1713	                                waitIfPaused
1714	                    waitIfPaused
1715	
1716	                    notifyProgress (SessionUpdated sess)
1717	                    ret <- agent1.step sess
1718	                    case ret of
1719	                        Stop _r ->
1720	                            -- smoll hack to reuse the naive step from nodeToAgent
1721	                            pure $ AskUserPrompt (MissingUserPrompt True [])
1722	                        _ -> pure ret
1723	                , usrQuery = do
1724	                    -- First check for buffered messages
1725	                    core <- readTVarIO coreRef
1726	                    buffered <- readAndClearBufferedMessages convId core
1727	                    case buffered of
1728	                        Nothing -> notifyNeedInput >> readBChan inChan
1729	                        Just buftxt -> pure (Just $ UserQuery buftxt [])
1730	                }
1731	
1732	    -- \* wrap in Conversation
1733	    let tuiAgent = TuiAgent (tuiAgentId baseTuiAgent) (tuiTree baseTuiAgent) (tuiNode baseTuiAgent) (tuiSlug baseTuiAgent)
1734	    threadId <- liftIO $ forkIO $ do
1735	        notifyProgress (SessionStarted session)
1736	        -- Loop.run now requires convId as first parameter
1737	        void $ Loop.run convId a session
1738	        notifyProgress (SessionCompleted session)
1739	    let conv =
1740	            Conversation
1741	                { conversationId = convId
1742	                , conversationAgent = tuiAgent
1743	                , conversationThreadId = Just threadId
1744	                , conversationSession = Nothing
1745	                , conversationName = "@" <> tuiSlug baseTuiAgent
1746	                , conversationChan = inChan
1747	                , conversationStatus = ConversationStatus_WaitingForInput
1748	                , conversationOnProgress = notifyProgress
1749	                }
1750

content.content:
1450	    tools <- liftIO $ readTVarIO (osNodeTools $ tuiNode agent)
1451	    tuiUI . coreAgentTools %= updateAgentTools (tuiAgentId agent) tools
1452	  where
1453	    updateAgentTools :: AgentId -> [a] -> [(AgentId, [a])] -> [(AgentId, [a])]
1454	    updateAgentTools aid newTools =
1455	        ((aid, newTools) :) . filter ((/= aid) . fst)
1456	
1457	-- | Handle F5 key: Refresh tools for selected agent.
1458	handleRefreshTools :: EventM N TuiState ()
1459	handleRefreshTools = do
1460	    selected <- use (tuiUI . selectedAgentInfo)
1461	    case selected of
1462	        Just agent -> do
1463	            -- Read tools from the OS-native TVar
1464	            tools <- liftIO $ readTVarIO (osNodeTools $ tuiNode agent)
1465	            tuiUI . coreAgentTools %= updateAgentTools (tuiAgentId agent) tools
1466	            showStatus StatusInfo $ "Refreshed " <> Text.pack (show $ length tools) <> " tools"
1467	        Nothing -> showStatus StatusWarning "No agent selected"
1468	  where
1469	    updateAgentTools :: AgentId -> [a] -> [(AgentId, [a])] -> [(AgentId, [a])]
1470	    updateAgentTools aid newTools =
1471	        ((aid, newTools) :) . filter ((/= aid) . fst)
1472	
1473	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1474	-- Conversation Management
1475	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1476	
1477	-- | Create a new conversation from the selected agent.
1478	handleNewConversationFromEditor :: Tracer IO Trace -> EventM N TuiState ()
1479	handleNewConversationFromEditor tracer = do
1480	    selected <- use (tuiUI . agentList . to listSelectedElement)
1481	    case selected of
1482	        Just (_, baseTuiAgent) -> do
1483	            session <- liftIO (Session [] <$> newSessionId <*> pure Nothing <*> newTurnId <*> pure (Just 1))
1484	            runConversation tracer baseTuiAgent session
1485	        _ ->
1486	            pure ()
1487	
1488	{- | Continue a session restored from storage.
1489	This starts the agent loop with the restored session.
1490	-}
1491	handleRestoredConversation :: Tracer IO Trace -> EventM N TuiState ()
1492	handleRestoredConversation tracer = do
1493	    mSession <- use (tuiUI . sessionList . to listSelectedElement)
1494	    mAgent <- use (tuiUI . agentList . to listSelectedElement)
1495	    case (,) <$> mSession <*> mAgent of
1496	        Just ((_, session), (_, baseTuiAgent)) -> do
1497	            runConversation tracer baseTuiAgent session
1498	        _ ->
1499	            pure ()
1500	
1501	{- | Toggle pause/unpause for the currently selected conversation.
1502	Ctrl+E pauses/unpauses the conversation, blocking the step iteration.
1503	-}
1504	handleTogglePauseConversation :: EventM N TuiState ()
1505	handleTogglePauseConversation = do
1506	    mSelectedConv <- use (tuiUI . conversationList . to listSelectedElement)
1507	    case mSelectedConv of
1508	        Nothing -> showStatus StatusWarning "No conversation selected"
1509	        Just (_, conv) -> do
1510	            let convId = conversationId conv
1511	            coreRef <- use tuiCore
1512	            isPaused <- Set.member convId . corePausedConversations <$> liftIO (readTVarIO coreRef)
1513	            if isPaused
1514	                then do
1515	                    -- Unpause: remove from paused set and update status
1516	                    liftIO $ atomically $ modifyTVar coreRef $ \c ->
1517	                        c{corePausedConversations = Set.delete convId (corePausedConversations c)}
1518	                    updateConversationStatus convId ConversationStatus_WaitingForInput
1519	                    -- Clear queue selection when unpausing
1520	                    tuiUI . queuedMessagesFocus .= Nothing
1521	                    showStatus StatusInfo $ "Unpaused: " <> conversationName conv
1522	                else do
1523	                    -- Pause: add to paused set and update status
1524	                    liftIO $ atomically $ modifyTVar coreRef $ \c ->
1525	                        c{corePausedConversations = Set.insert convId (corePausedConversations c)}
1526	                    updateConversationStatus convId ConversationStatus_Paused
1527	                    showStatus StatusInfo $ "Paused: " <> conversationName conv
1528	
1529	-- | Check if a conversation is currently paused.
1530	isConversationPaused :: ConversationId -> Core -> Bool
1531	isConversationPaused convId core = Set.member convId (corePausedConversations core)
1532	
1533	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1534	-- Queue Management
1535	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1536	
1537	{- | Clear all queued messages for the current conversation.
1538	Only works when the conversation is paused.
1539	-}
1540	handleClearQueuedMessages :: EventM N TuiState ()
1541	handleClearQueuedMessages = do
1542	    mConv <- getFocusedConversation
1543	    case mConv of
1544	        Nothing -> showStatus StatusWarning "No conversation selected"
1545	        Just conv -> do
1546	            -- Only allow clearing when paused
1547	            if conversationStatus conv /= ConversationStatus_Paused
1548	                then showStatus StatusWarning "Can only clear queued messages when paused (Ctrl+E)"
1549	                else do
1550	                    let convId = conversationId conv
